trivia_bullets.text: Modern Horizons 3 (released June 14, 2024) brought the first new Eldrazi cards since Eldritch Moon (2016). The MH3 Eldrazi suite centers on Emrakul — the only Titan not destroyed, sealed inside Innistrad's moon — and includes Emrakul, the World Anew as its centerpiece. Hope-Ender Coatl sits in the brood tier: smaller Eldrazi creatures that are extensions of the Titans' hunger.
sources: WebSearch: Dexerto — MTG fans react to eldritch Eldrazi return in Modern Horizons 3

trivia_bullets.text: The 'Coatl' name invokes the Mesoamerican feathered serpent (Quetzalcoatl) and aligns with existing MTG cards like Lorescale Coatl (Alara Reborn) and River Heralds coatls on Ixalan — all typed as Snakes in the rules. MH3's card is an Eldrazi Snake, meaning Wizards used 'Coatl' as the flavor-name for an Eldrazi whose base organism was a coatl-type creature. No creature type named 'Coatl' exists in the rules — a Scryfall search for t:coatl returns zero results.

Devoid (no color despite blue mana cost), Flash, Flying, and a cast trigger that functions as a conditional counterspell: target spell countered unless opponent pays {1}. The trigger resolves before the Coatl enters the battlefield — if the Coatl is countered in response, the trigger still resolves. MH3's brood-corruption-of-subtype pattern: this card is Snake, sibling Twisted Riddlekeeper (MH3-14) is Sphinx, Snapping Voidcraw (MH3-204) is Turtle. EDHREC rank: 11,217.
sources: Scryfall: MH3-64 oracle_text, keywords, frame_effects: devoid
WebSearch: MTGAssist ruling snippet — trigger resolves before creature

trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by Filip Burburan. The card carries Scryfall frame_effects: ['devoid'] — confirming the colorless card frame treatment applied to Devoid cards in MH3.
